data.body:
MANUAL PLAN-REVIEW (5fd2 three-pass) of EACH of the 6 children of epic 8f6c (code-grounding oracle).

== PROCESS == Children are STORIES, so applies_at selected the STORY subset (22 criteria: + E5/T5d/T6/T9 vs
the epic run; EXCLUDED container G3/G4 (no children) and task-only leaf E6/G1G2/A1/E4/T5a-c). Three-pass per child:
Pass-1 = 6 independent finder agents (one per child, verbatim PASS1_SYSTEM, story rubric, full artifact for
absence/cross-ref, repo grounding for AGENT-tier T1/G6/T3/T8/T10/T11). Pass-2 = 6 independent verifiers (fresh
context, verbatim PASS2_SYSTEM, charitable plan-reading + complete-artifact absence bar, repo-grounded). Pass-3 =
deterministic (S1/S2 verifiers emitted the full graded set -> exact confidence; S3-S6 emitted a reduced set ->
veto + evidence_entails + severity). DET floor P1-P8 passes/fail-open for all 6 (all pass clarity+check_ac).

== RESULT == 23 Pass-1 findings -> 15 ADVISORY + 8 DROPPED, 0 BLOCKS. Per-child advisory: S1=0, S2=2, S3=1,
S4=6, S5=4, S6=2. S1 is the cleanest (all 3 findings dropped on charitable/altitude grounds). S4 (Engine B) drew
the most (most complex story). Verifier filter rate 8/23 (~35%), consistent with the anti-FP posture.

== CONVERGENT THEMES (the actionable clusters) ==
1. UNDEFINED CONTRACT/IDENTIFIER VOCABULARIES (dominant — hit independently by the S2, S4 AND S5 finders):
   - reference-in `kind` value set never enumerated (S2#0, major; conf .50) — AC4 'defined+validated' can't be met.
   - dimension-ID vocabulary defined nowhere (S4#3 + S5#0, minor) — S4 detectors declare a dimension; S5 only
     'documents' it; no child defines the closed set, so 5fd2/9da1 can't route deterministically.
   - `evidence-mapping` envelope field undefined AND the prose 6-field envelope doesn't reconcile with the
     spike's concrete 4-field rebar_envelope (S4#2, major).
   - `scan(...)` signature left as an ellipsis on the public API (S5#1, minor).
   FIX: enumerate the `kind` set + the dimension-ID set (own it in S2/S5 resp.), define `evidence-mapping`'s
   shape (reconcile with the spike envelope), give `scan` a typed signature.
2. OPENGREP FAILURE-MODE COVERAGE (both major):
   - loader pre-validation not specified ENGINE-FAITHFUL — must use opengrep/semgrep `--validate`, else a rule
     rebar accepts but the engine rejects re-trips the exit-7 whole-run abort (S4#0).
   - the abort/invalid-detector behavior is proven only for OpenGrep; ast-grep + scc/lizard (metric tools with
     no rule schema) are assumed by analogy, yet AC6 requires invalid-detector->abstain 'per backend' (S4#5).
   FIX: pin validation to the engine's own checker; establish each backend's invalid-input failure mode (scc/
   lizard 'invalid-detector' likely n/a — say so).
3. THE EVAL VALIDATES ONLY THE EASY SYMBOL CLASS (S6 major + S3):
   - S6 corpus omits the exact false-refute hazard the epic/spike named — common-name COLLISIONS and member/
     attribute/SIGNATURE resolution — so 'false-refute = 0' is attested only on distinct names the spike already
     showed safe (S6#0, major).
   - the yield eval is seeded ONLY by the self-authored spike fixture -> a self-fulfilling number, not the
     'measured on a real corpus' the story promises (S6#1, major).
   - the DEPS lane (S3) has zero empirical validation (the spike is symbol-only); its eval is deferred to the
     symbol-seeded S6 (S3#0, minor).
   FIX: add collision + member/signature fixtures (assert they ABSTAIN, not refute); source the yield corpus
   from independent real repos with independent labels; add a deps-lane eval (real manifests across ecosystems).
4. SMALLER ADVISORIES: S2#1 title still reads 'grep-ast/ctags T1 floor' (stale vs the revised body — a one-line
   fix; an artifact of the R-A edit). S4#1 detector-registry build/cache/invalidation/concurrency lifecycle
   unspecified (minor; process-local state). S4#4 S4-internal 3-backend sequencing not covered by the epic's
   cross-engine #4 override (minor). S5#3 no AC for the canonical .schema.json + OUTPUT_SCHEMAS registration +
   conformance-test convention S5 claims to mirror (minor; CI self-enforces it). S5#4 T7 docs-invalidation: new
   read tools make CLAUDE.md's MCP Reads list + docs/output-schemas.md stale; no AC to refresh them (minor).

== DROPPED (8, informational — Pass-2 charitably ruled them satisfied/covered/altitude) ==
S1#0 optionality gate is name-enumerated but extending it is entailed by AC3's 'gate passes' (conf .36);
S1#1 segfault is covered by the worker-subprocess boundary, 'same reaper' is loose phrasing (conf .29);
S1#2 a crash can map to the enum's explicit `other` catch-all (conf .29); S3#1 gauntlet steps named + bs4
example given, endpoints are task-leaf; S3#2 cache TTL is task-leaf, refute only on positive existence; S3#3 an
uncited motivation premise is normal at ticket altitude + safe-abstain neutralizes it; S5#2 'stable'/cross-epic
adoption is inherent to contract work, the checkable core is evaluable; S6#2 real-vs-fixture wording resolves
to one artifact under a reasonable reading (restatement of S6#1).

== META == The gate found MORE on the children (15) than on the epic (6) — expected: stories carry concrete
contract detail to scrutinize + the story rubric adds E5/T9. The identifier-vocabulary gap converging across
three independent finders is the strongest signal. The gate also caught a real defect I introduced last round
(S2's stale title). Recommended: apply cluster-1/2/3 fixes + the four smaller advisories; the 8 dropped need no
action. (Self-review caveat: I authored these children, so this is self-review; the independent finder/verifier
split + deterministic gate is the mitigation, same as the epic round.)
